data.description:

Apex Ace Holding Ltd. ( (HK:6036) ) just unveiled an update.

Apex Ace Holding Limited has called its annual general meeting for 22 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the accompanying directors’ and auditor’s reports. The agenda also includes re‑election of board members, approval of directors’ and auditor’s remuneration, re‑appointment of CCTH CPA Limited as independent auditor, and authorisation for the board to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued shares, which may influence capital structure and shareholder returns.

The share repurchase mandate, if approved, would permit the company to buy back its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange or other recognised exchanges during the period until the next AGM or expiry of the statutory timeframe. This flexibility may allow Apex Ace to manage its equity base more actively, potentially supporting its share price and signalling confidence in its financial position while reinforcing existing governance arrangements through confirmed board and auditor appointments.

More about Apex Ace Holding Ltd.

Apex Ace Holding Limited is a Hong Kong‑listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and operates in the technology sector. The group is overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non‑executive directors, and its shares are traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong.

Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2409) ) has issued an update.

Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. has called its annual general meeting for 26 June 2026 in Qingdao, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the reports of the board and auditor. They will also vote on board changes, including the re-election of an independent non-executive director, the appointment of two new executive directors, and authorization for the board to set directors’ pay, alongside the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor.

Shareholders will further consider granting the board general mandates to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued shares, including the ability to hold repurchased shares as treasury stock, and to issue additional shares or dispose of treasury shares within defined limits. These mandates, if approved, would give the board additional flexibility in capital management and equity issuance during the mandate period, potentially influencing the company’s capital structure and shareholder value.

More about Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd.

Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and engaged in the shipping sector. Its shares trade on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 2409, and it operates with a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors.

Riverine China Holdings Limited ( (HK:1417) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Riverine China Holdings Limited has revised the terms of reference of its Nomination Committee, originally established in 2017, to align with updated Hong Kong corporate governance requirements on board diversity and committee composition. The company has appointed Ms Wang Hui to the Nomination Committee effective 28 April 2026 to satisfy the requirement that at least one member be of a different gender, and confirmed that the committee will comprise five directors, with a majority being independent non-executive directors, tasked with director nominations, board performance evaluation support, and succession planning.

These changes reinforce the company’s commitment to fair and transparent director appointments and to maintaining a diverse and independent board structure in line with the Corporate Governance Code. By formalizing the Nomination Committee’s role in succession planning and board evaluation, Riverine China Holdings seeks to underpin its long-term success and enhance governance standards, which may bolster confidence among investors and other stakeholders.

More about Riverine China Holdings Limited

Riverine China Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1417, operates under the corporate governance framework of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The company is overseen by a board of directors and board committees, including a Nomination Committee that manages director selection and succession planning to support its long-term development.

data.title: BYD Strengthens Corporate Governance With New Nomination Committee Rules
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from BYD Co ( (HK:1211) ) is now available.

BYD Company Limited has formalized detailed implementation rules for its Nomination Committee, a special committee under the board of directors tasked with overseeing the selection and governance of directors, the president and other senior officers. The rules define the committee’s composition, requiring at least three directors with a majority of independent non-executive directors, gender diversity, and an independent non-executive director as chairman, with terms aligned to those of the board.

Operational procedures are set out for convening meetings, voting, record-keeping and the use of teleconferencing and written resolutions, while ensuring adequate information and resources are provided to the committee. The Nomination Committee is mandated to annually review the board’s structure, size and skills mix, evaluate the independence of independent directors, and develop standards and procedures for senior appointments and succession planning, strengthening BYD’s corporate governance and board oversight as the company grows.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1211) stock is a Buy with a HK$22.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BYD Co stock, see the HK:1211 Stock Forecast page.

BYD Company Limited is a Chinese conglomerate operating primarily in the automotive and new energy sectors, best known globally for its electric vehicles and rechargeable batteries. The company has been expanding its international footprint while strengthening internal governance structures to support fast-growing operations and sustain competitiveness in global markets.

Datang International Power Generation Co ( (HK:0991) ) has shared an update.

Datang International’s board met in Beijing on 28 April 2026, with all 15 directors present, and unanimously approved the company’s first-quarter 2026 report after prior review by its audit committee. Tianjin Port Development Holdings ( (HK:3382) ) has shared an announcement.

Tianjin Port Development Holdings has disclosed the unaudited first-quarter 2026 results of its mainland subsidiary, Tianjin Port Holdings, prepared under Chinese accounting standards. The parent indirectly owns about 56.81% of the Shanghai-listed port operator, which reported a modest contraction in revenue but a notable improvement in profitability.

data.title: BYD’s First-Quarter Profit Slumps as Revenue and Cash Flow Weaken
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BYD Co ( (HK:1211) ) has issued an announcement.

BYD Company Limited reported a sharp downturn in first-quarter 2026 results, with operating revenue falling 11.82% year on year to RMB150.23 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders tumbling 55.38% to RMB4.08 billion. Earnings per share halved, return on net assets dropped to 1.65%, and operating cash flow slid 67.48%, though total assets and shareholders’ equity edged up slightly, reflecting balance-sheet expansion despite pressure on profitability.

The weaker performance underscores significant margin and cash flow compression for the group in the latest quarter, suggesting a more challenging operating environment after a period of rapid growth. While BYD’s asset and equity bases continued to grow modestly, the steep declines in profit and cash generation may sharpen investor focus on cost control, pricing power, and market dynamics in its core automotive and technology businesses.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1211) stock is a Buy with a HK$22.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BYD Co stock, see the HK:1211 Stock Forecast page.

More about BYD Co

BYD Company Limited is a Chinese joint stock company listed in Hong Kong and Shenzhen that operates in the automotive and related technology sectors. The group is known for its large-scale manufacturing capabilities and focuses on vehicles and associated products for both domestic and international markets.

Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. Class H ( (HK:2429) ) has shared an announcement.

Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. has convened its 2025 annual general meeting for May 28, 2026 in Shenzhen, where shareholders will review the company’s 2025 annual report, board work report, profit distribution plan, and consolidated financial statements, as well as the financial budget for 2026. Investors will also vote on reappointing PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, adjusting remuneration for key executives including the chairman and CFO, withdrawing a prior CSRC application tied to a Pre-IPO incentive scheme, and granting the board a broad share issuance and treasury share disposal mandate that could influence future capital management and shareholder dilution.

The agenda underscores routine governance items alongside strategic capital and incentive decisions that may affect the company’s financial flexibility and executive alignment with shareholder interests. The proposed general mandate to issue additional shares and deal with treasury shares aims to give the board more room to respond to financing needs or market opportunities within Hong Kong listing rules, while the withdrawal of the Pre-IPO share incentive application signals a shift in the company’s approach to equity-based compensation and regulatory engagement in mainland China.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shenzhen Expressway Co ( (HK:0548) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shenzhen Expressway reported unaudited toll revenue figures for March 2026 across its portfolio of expressway projects, led by key assets such as the Outer Ring Project, Coastal Project and Jihe corridors in the Shenzhen region, as well as significant contributions from Qinglian and GS Expressways in other parts of Guangdong. The disclosure, which details revenues by project and average daily intake, is intended as interim statistical information, with the board cautioning investors that figures may differ from future audited data due to estimation methods and settlement adjustments, underscoring that the numbers should be used carefully for investment decisions.

The company also highlighted that some toll revenue data are affected by inter-network toll collection and subsequent reconciliations, meaning monthly forecasts may be revised as actual settlement data become available. This approach reflects both operational complexity in China’s toll road network and the company’s practice of updating forecasts, signaling that short-term revenue swings at the project level may not fully capture underlying long-term traffic and cash flow trends for stakeholders monitoring its performance.

More about Shenzhen Expressway Co

Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited is a toll road operator in the People’s Republic of China, focusing on the development, operation and management of expressways and related infrastructure. The company’s core assets are concentrated in Guangdong province, particularly in the Shenzhen region, with additional projects in other parts of Guangdong and several other Chinese provinces.

data.title: Qeeka Home switches auditor from PwC to BDO after fee dispute
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc. ( (HK:1739) ) has shared an announcement.

Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc. has replaced PricewaterhouseCoopers as its independent auditor after failing to reach an agreement on sharply higher audit fees linked to the group’s fast-growing international service operations. The board and audit committee determined that PwC’s proposed 2025 fee of RMB4 million, up from RMB2.8 million in 2024, exceeded the company’s budget, and invited tenders from other firms before selecting BDO with a proposal of RMB2.48 million.

The company emphasized that PwC’s resignation stemmed solely from the fee dispute rather than any issues with audit quality or governance, framing it as a mutually recognized impasse rather than a forced termination. BDO has since been appointed auditor and will conduct the 2025 audit under International Standards on Auditing using its global audit methodology, a move that may lower costs while maintaining compliance amid Qeeka’s growing international complexity.

More about Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc.

Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc. is a Hong Kong-listed company that operates through a group structure and is incorporated in the Cayman Islands with limited liability. The group has been rapidly expanding its International Service Business, which has increased its operational scale, geographical reach and the complexity of its accounting and financial reporting requirements.

The agenda also includes the re-election of executive and independent non-executive directors, confirmation of directors’ remuneration, and the re-appointment of BDO Limited as auditor.

Shareholders will further be asked to approve a share repurchase mandate allowing the board to buy back up to 10% of the company’s issued shares, as well as a general mandate to issue additional shares and grant related options and awards within prescribed limits. These authorities, if granted, would give the board greater flexibility in capital management and financing, potentially affecting capital structure, shareholder returns and the company’s ability to respond to market opportunities over the coming year.

More about Frontage Holdings Corp.

Frontage Holdings Corporation is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the life sciences and pharmaceutical-related services sector. The company provides research, development and related scientific services to biopharmaceutical clients, positioning itself within the broader contract research and development industry focused on drug discovery and development support.

An update from Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited Class H ( (HK:1171) ) is now available.

Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited has proposed a new slate of directors for its tenth board session, including six directors and four independent non-executive director candidates, with their eligibility and independence for certain roles subject to review by the Shanghai Stock Exchange. The candidates, along with an employee representative director elected separately by staff, will be put to shareholders for approval at the 2025 annual general meeting, with three-year terms starting from the conclusion of that meeting.

The company also announced that several current directors, including one independent non-executive director who has reached the six-year regulatory limit, will step down at the end of the ninth board session, prompting a refresh of key board and committee positions. The outgoing directors have indicated they have no disagreements with the board or issues requiring shareholder attention, and the company has expressed its appreciation for their service as it prepares for the next governance cycle.
